mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved economic activity.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no correlation (Rank IC < 0.005) with industrial production changes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite inflation spikes, or the negative predictive relationship disappears when inflation exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to translate into equity gains when accompanied by margin compression, or the 60-day return correlation with GDP growth falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive despite industrial production contractions, or the threshold signal shows a false positive rate exceeding 70% in backtesting.
